ListItem.Para: Predictive windshear alert mode operates normally.
Alternate procedures are established and used.
Repairs are made within two flight days.
PM must maintain a heightened awareness of the altitude and flight path of the aircraft and alert the PF if flight parameters are out of normal tolerance.
Be especially alert for and announce:
Excessive terrain closure rate.
Excessive descent rate.
Altitude loss after takeoff or go-around.
Unsafe terrain clearance.
Deviation from glideslope.
Flight crew should use all systems available (autopilot, autoland, ILS and FMF), approach altitude callouts, and vertical situation display (VSD).
Review FCOM supplementary procedure for windshear and QRH Manoeuvres Windshear guidance.
RNP AR APCH are not to be conducted.
Alternate procedures are established and used.
Repairs are made within two flight days.
PM must maintain a heightened awareness of the altitude and flight path of the aircraft and alert the PF if flight parameters are out of normal tolerance.
Be especially alert for and announce:
Excessive terrain closure rate.
Excessive descent rate.
Altitude loss after takeoff or go-around.
Unsafe terrain clearance.
Deviation from glideslope.
Flight crew should use all systems available (autopilot, autoland, ILS and FMF), approach altitude callouts, and vertical situation display (VSD).
Review FCOM supplementary procedure for windshear and QRH Manoeuvres Windshear guidance.
RNP AR APCH are not to be conducted.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are established and used.

ListItem.Para: PM must maintain a heightened awareness of the altitude and flight path of the aircraft and alert the PF if flight parameters are out of normal tolerance.
Flight crew should use all systems available (autopilot, autoland, ILS and FMF), approach altitude callouts, and vertical situation display (VSD).
Alternate procedures are established and used.
Predictive windshear alert mode operates normally.
Review FCOM supplementary procedure for windshear and QRH Manoeuvres Windshear guidance.
PM must maintain a heightened awareness of the altitude and flight path of the aircraft and alert the PF if flight parameters are out of normal tolerance.
Be especially alert for and announce:
Excessive terrain closure rate.
Excessive descent rate.
Altitude loss after takeoff or go-around.
Unsafe terrain clearance.
Deviation from glideslope.

Note.Para: The WINDSHEAR SYS advisory message will be displayed.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are established and used.

ListItem.Para: Review FCOM supplementary procedure for windshear and QRH Manoeuvres Windshear guidance.
PM must maintain a heightened awareness of the altitude and flight path of the aircraft and alert the PF if flight parameters are out of normal tolerance.
Be especially alert for and announce:
Excessive terrain closure rate.
Excessive descent rate.
Altitude loss after takeoff or go-around.
Unsafe terrain clearance.
Deviation from glideslope.

Note.Para: The WINDSHEAR SYS advisory message will be displayed.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are established and used.

ListItem.Para: PM must maintain a heightened awareness of the charted terrain relative to the current and projected flight path of the aircraft and ensure the PF is aware of significant terrain in the area.
Flight crew should use all systems available (autopilot, autoland, ILS and FMF), approach altitude callouts, and vertical situation display (VSD).
RNP AR APCH are not to be conducted.
